title: Korlai Fort
tagline: Korlai Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Korlai Fort is a colonial fort in the Raigad district of Maharashtra, India.
overview.description: Korlai Fort is a colonial fort in the Raigad district of Maharashtra, India. Formerly a part of Chaul in Portuguese India, the structure is a specimen of Portuguese colonial architecture. It was built as a companion to the fort at Chaul, at this strategic position the Portuguese could use it to defend their province which stretched from Korlai to the Fort Bassein. Vestiges of the Portuguese occupation are manifested in the distinct dialect of the Korlai villages inhabitants which is a Luso-Indian creole called Korlai Portuguese Creole.

topPlaces.name: Chaul
topPlaces.description: Chaul is a village in the Alibag taluka of the Raigad district in Maharashtra, India. It was a town of Portuguese India, with the Korlai Fort located nearby.

topPlaces.name: Revdanda
topPlaces.description: Revdanda is a village near Alibaug, Raigad District, Maharashtra. It is the site of the medieval 'Chaul harbor'.

topPlaces.name: Diwiparangi
topPlaces.description: Diviparangi is a village situated in south-east of Alibag taluka in Raigad district, Maharashtra, India, on a creek of the Arabian Sea. By road the village is 16 km from the city of Alibag.

topPlaces.name: Navghar-Manikpur
topPlaces.description: Navghar-Manikpur is a city and a municipal council in Thane district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

items.note: Chaul is a village in the Alibag taluka of the Raigad district in Maharashtra, India. It was a town of Portuguese India, with the Korlai Fort located nearby.
Revdanda is a village near Alibaug, Raigad District, Maharashtra. It is the site of the medieval 'Chaul harbor'.
Diviparangi is a village situated in south-east of Alibag taluka in Raigad district, Maharashtra, India, on a creek of the Arabian Sea. By road the village is 16 km from the city of Alibag.
Navghar-Manikpur is a city and a municipal council in Thane district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Korlai Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Korlai Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Korlai Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport, Mumbai (62 km) and the nearest railway station is Mumbai CSMT (46 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Korlai Fort?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Korlai Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Korlai Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Korlai Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Korlai Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Chaul, Revdanda, Diwiparangi, Navghar-Manikpur.
